name: Chicken Tetrazzini[w/o Soup]
recipeInstructions:
1. In a Dutch oven, combine the chicken with enough salted water to cover it by 2 inches. Bring the water to a boil and simmer the chicken for 20 minutes, or until tender. Let the chicken cool in the broth, then separate the meat from the skin and bones, returning the skin and bones to the broth. Cut the meat into strips and reserve.
2. Reduce the broth by half, strain through a fine sieve, discard solids and skim fat, then boil until reduced to about 2 cups.
3. In a large saucepan, cook mushrooms in 2 tablespoons butter over moderately low heat until softened.
4. Boil spaghetti in salted water until al dente, drain well.
5. Melt remaining 3 tablespoons butter in a saucepan over moderately low heat. Add flour and cook roux for 3 minutes.
6. Whisk reduced broth, cream, and sherry into the sauce, bring to boil, and simmer for 5 minutes. Season with nutmeg, salt, and pepper.
7. Stir half the sauce into mushrooms with spaghetti. Add reserved chicken to remaining sauce, combine well, and transfer to center of spaghetti mixture.
8. Sprinkle with Parmesan and bake in preheated 350°F (175°C) oven for 25-30 minutes or until pale golden.
